notes: Generally you can play it. From what I figured out you need to install Proton via tools section explicitly and not just enabling it. After that you'll be able to go past black screen in the start. Runs ok but not ideal. Some issues: Can crash randomly, either during fight or a cutscene. This is especially annoying after a boss fight. The slow mo effect can extend to actual gameplay (killed Artemis boss with like 5 fps...). Sometimes pausing helps, and sometimes doesn't. Will not launch with PROTON_USE_WINED3D. Disabling ESYNC has no effect so don't bother.

The game runs wonderful but I have a pretty strong system. The Denuvo performance tax can be a issue for low end CPUs I recommend searching in the web the patched Denuvo free exe

notes: Finally got the mfplat.dll workaround to work (as outlined roughly in this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pcCJSm3IoMY (I know it says its for resident evil 2, but it works for DMC5 as well, you just need to change the steamapp id when dealing with your proton prefix. title: Devil May Cry 5

notes: As Stated, the solution for RE2 in this video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pcCJSm3IoMY&t=163s does work for fixing the cutscene crash at the end, but an important detail is that a recent proton update integrates x-audio, so DO NOT install xact through winetricks like the video instructs, only install mf. xact will cause a crash on attempt of starting the game. After that, game works perfectly! Giving gold rather than plat. due to these needed tweeks

title: Devil May Cry 5

notes: Awesome !
title: Devil May Cry 5

notes:
As described in other posts, a similar procedure as to get RE2 running is needed. Just had to manually install mf through winetricks and then copied a Windows 7 mfplat.dll to the game folder using these commands:
> WIN7LIBPATH="/path/to/win7libs/" STEAMAPPSPATH="/path/to/steamapps/" #replace with your own paths
> WINEPREFIX="$STEAMAPPSPATH/compatdata/601150/pfx/" winetricks mf
> cp "$WIN7LIBPATH/mfplat.dll" "$STEAMAPPSPATH/common/Devil May Cry 5/mfplat.dll"
After that it works as good as native, Steam Controller and Big Picture included.

notes: Requires an MFPlat.dll and the workaround on this video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pcCJSm3IoMY after doing that the game works flawlessly. If you want to skip the MFPlat/Virtual machine part on tutorial this MFPlat file: (https://www.opendll.com/index.php?file-download=mfplat.dll&arch=64bit&version=12.0.7600.16385#) is new enough and works flawlessly that the skill shop videos play etc. Make sure you don't use an older MFPlat or it will crash the game when you go to view skill shop previews. I know from experience. Good luck slaying those demons.

notes: Finished the whole game on Linux. Is perfectly playable with impressive performance (although cinematics could be problematic if graphics are set too high, with lags that create a gap between audio and video). Only two issues to notice: one minor, is that videos in the skill section of customization are not loading, so you can't see the skill you are buying in action (but can still test it), the other more important because the final cinematic at the end of the game doesn't play. So you can't see the end, and the game doesn't register the fact that you finished it (regarding achievements).

title: Devil May Cry 5

notes:
The game run out of the box with perfect graphics, audio and controls. The performance is good but  it's noticeable slower than windows. My system runs in 4K Max settings on W10, on linux i have to downgrade to 1800p and use SSAO instead HBAO+ to get 60fps. As already described here, the game needs mplat.dll to play cutscenes and tutorial videos, it s almost the same method as RE2, the diference is that you do not need the xact lib, only mf will do the trick. The following tutorial works for me:
http://bestlinuxgames.com/?q=content/devil-may-cry-5-proton-fix-mfplatdll

title: Devil May Cry 5

notes: I don't know how people can rate this platinum or gold. If you don't have the mfplat.dll workaround, the game will crash whenever you try to upgrade your abilites and you hover over those. The video wants to start playing, it can't. As soon as you want to leave the upgrade screen, the game crashes. Audio desync in cutscenes if the fps drop. Not a huge deal but it's still an issue. Animation speed seems to be tied to the fps. So if your fps go down to 30 fps, the game runs at half speed. At this time I really can't recommend getting it. At least until the mfplat.dll issue is fixed. But even then it's hardly enjoyable if the animation speed varies like crazy.

title: Devil May Cry 5

notes:
Gold and Plat ratings are extremely misleading. Game crashes due to mfplat in upgrade scene, post-credits cutscene and I tried the fix for mfplat: it doesn't work and another linux friend of mine who tried it with no success, too. Cutscenes have desync'd audio/video. 

Until there's a native fix for the mfplat issue this game is woefully misrepresented because any DMC fans knows the real game starts AFTER the credits where you grind out to become the best - not something you can do with the game in this state. Patiently awaiting stronger proton to handle malarkey like this.
title: Devil May Cry 5
